ordered_steps.name: Load approved P3-SPEC-PATTERNS review, pattern ids, presentation decisions, and exceptions
Bind canonical element projection and approved interaction message authority
Write canonical layout prose covering all layout coverage groups
Define desktop, tablet, mobile, iOS, and Material carrier adaptation rules
Define multi-level containment hierarchy with order and reflow coupling
Define content growth and information completeness rules for height, width, and horizontal scroll exceptions
Define state placement and z-axis layering with Material elevation intent
Bind layout package through composition index and non-authoritative Figma reconstruction metadata
Validate schema alignment, reference integrity, carrier coverage, information completeness, and scope

layout_rules: Natural language layout prose is canonical semantic authority; structured records are index and validation skeleton only.
Carrier profile must include DESKTOP, TABLET, MOBILE, MOBILE_IOS, and MOBILE_MATERIAL.
MOBILE_IOS must include safe area, system bars, navigation stack, and keyboard avoidance constraints.
MOBILE_MATERIAL must include system bars, app bar or navigation expectations, keyboard inset, dialog or snackbar distinction, and Material elevation intent.
Containment must be multi-level and child order must preserve semantic sequence under carrier reflow.
Child width and scroll behavior cannot contradict parent constraints.
Height limits cannot hide, omit, remove, or drop required information; vertical access or recovery path is mandatory.
Width limits must wrap, stack, adapt density, or declare a horizontal scroll exception for wide structures.
Every approved interaction message requiring layout must have a state placement record.
Z-axis layers must be ordered, unique, and blocking behavior must be explicit for interactive layers.
Figma metadata is reconstruction guidance only and cannot introduce write authority or semantic drift.
Generic schema optional refs become P3-required bindings for pattern_refs, state_variants, z_axis_refs, figma_metadata_ref, and composition z_axis_refs.
Layout cannot create product capability or element scope beyond approved upstream decisions.
forbidden_behaviors: make inventory or Figma metadata the canonical layout authority
omit iOS or Material mobile constraints when mobile is supported
flatten containment into a single page node when nested surfaces exist
hide or drop required information because viewport height is short
ignore width or clip required information without recovery
allow offscreen overflow without a declared horizontal scroll exception
use Material overlay or modal behavior without material elevation intent
place unknown interaction messages or unknown surfaces
omit P3-required optional refs such as pattern_refs, state_variants, z_axis_refs, figma_metadata_ref, or composition z_axis_refs
write repository implementation code during this spec workpack
